prompt: Develop a PantryPilot Offline Meal Kit Booking & Recipe Operations system for a regional food retailer that sells recipe-based meal kits for scheduled pickup. Customers use an English Layui web interface on in-store kiosks or a local Wi-Fi portal to sign in with username and password, search recipes with smart filters (ingredients, prep time under 30 minutes, step count, cookware, difficulty, calorie and allergen tags, and budget such as under $15.00), and get ranked results by popular, time-saving, budget, or low-calorie. Search must support synonyms and fuzzy matching so garbanzo returns chickpea, and minor typos still find matches. From a recipe, the customer selects a pickup point and books a time slot with immediate UI feedback on remaining capacity; the system enforces a booking window of up to 7 days ahead and a cutoff of 2 hours before pickup. Store Staff use an operations console to monitor today’s pickups and mark arrivals; a booking is considered a no-show if not checked in within 15 minutes of the slot start, and repeated no-shows drive automated blacklisting. Operations Managers configure homepage modules (carousel banners, campaign slots, hot rankings) and message templates, and review dashboards for booking conversion, slot utilization, no-show rate, and payment success. Finance Admins review payment and reconciliation screens, while System Administrators manage accounts, roles, and permissions across stores and departments. The backend uses ThinkPHP to expose REST-style APIs to the Layui frontend over a local network, persisting all business data in MySQL (recipes, tags, bookings, pickup points, campaigns, message events, and audit logs). Identity supports JWT or server sessions, password rules require at least 10 characters with one letter and one number, and accounts lock for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts; authorization follows a three-layer role-permission-resource model with data scoping by store/warehouse/department. Payment processing implements a locally hosted WeChat Pay-compatible gateway emulator for offline lanes: order placement creates a pending payment that auto-cancels after 10 minutes, callbacks must pass signature verification (HMAC-SHA256) and are processed idempotently using the transaction reference, with daily on-device reconciliation that flags missed orders and supports abnormal state repair. Fund-related actions (refund, adjustment, reconciliation close) require re-authentication and are logged with tamper-evident hashes. Address and service-area validation runs fully offline using stored ZIP+4 and admin-region tables plus distance calculation (Haversine) to enforce, for example, a 8-mile dispatch radius; route output is a printable dispatch note rather than any external navigation API. Notifications are delivered via an in-app message center with opt-out, max 2 marketing messages per day, quiet hours from 9:00 PM-8:00 AM, and delivery analytics based on reads and clicks. File governance stores attachments on local disk with strict type/size validation (for example JPG/PNG/PDF up to 10 MB), magic-byte checks and SHA-256 fingerprints, optional image watermarking, authenticated downloads via short-lived signed URLs (5-minute expiry), lifecycle cleanup after 180 days, and hotlink protection through token validation; sensitive fields (address, phone) are encrypted at rest and masked in UI and exports, while reports export to local CSV with anomaly alerts (oversell, refund-rate spikes, stockout rate) surfaced inside the ops console.
